dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic took the day on breadth: HN had a clearly useful Claude Code workflow thread, a separate comparison comment preferring Opus and Claude Code, and only one bug thread dragging sentiment back [E3][E5][E4].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic won because Claude-related discussion showed up across more meaningful stories than any other tracked provider, and two of those signals were favorable: developers said Claude Code workflows were useful in practice and one comparison thread explicitly preferred Opus 4.5 and Claude Code's UX. The broken CLI release was a real negative, but it was one contained incident against otherwise constructive product discussion, leaving Anthropic with the strongest mix of positive sentiment and coverage [E3][E5][E4].

openai.judgementSnippet: ChatGPT Health drew several firsthand reports that ChatGPT helped with chronic-condition debugging and medical-data interpretation, but a prominent comment described ChatGPT wrongly assuming a user had ADHD for months, and the Google-vs-OpenAI story framed OpenAI as losing clear separation from rivals [E1][E2].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Developers treated Claude Code as a productive workflow they were actively copying, and one comparison thread said Opus 4.5 and Claude Code felt better than Gemini alternatives; the main drag was a separate thread where a Claude Code CLI release was called embarrassing for breaking basic startup behavior [E3][E5][E4].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: The Google-vs-OpenAI story title gave Gemini a lift by presenting it as having pulled ahead, but top comments pushed back, calling the piece PR puffery and arguing Gemini still lagged on creativity and multi-step problem solving [E6].

evidence.summary: In the ChatGPT Health thread, several top-level comments said ChatGPT had been genuinely helpful for symptom triage, interpreting test results, and surfacing possible explanations that doctors later checked.
The Google-versus-OpenAI story title said Google had edged ahead, and a top comment called the piece puffery but still said it showed OpenAI no longer had a clear moat.
The Claude Code setup thread included users saying they adopted parts of the workflow, found the follow-up explanations useful, and were happily running multiple Claude Code sessions in parallel.
The Claude Code CLI breakage thread was sharply critical, with comments saying the failure was embarrassing, should have been caught by basic testing, and exposed shaky permissions/config behavior.
In the Google AI comparison thread, a top comment said Opus 4.5 could do much more than Gemini and that Claude Code felt smoother to use than Gemini CLI.

The story itself framed Google Gemini as having regained momentum and moved ahead of OpenAI, though commenters heavily qualified that claim by saying the article felt like PR and Gemini still lacked wit and strong multi-step reasoning.
